text: There’s a bracing chill in the air as I pause in this glass-walled corridor, watching a figure contemplate an exhibit marked 'R+D.' The space feels modern, a testament to human ingenuity—simultaneously inviting and sterile, blending curiosity with a quiet sense of vulnerability. Surrounded by the echoes of innovation and the promise of intelligent machines, I find myself pondering the paradox of progress: does greater knowledge always bring us closer to one another, or might it also drive new forms of isolation?
